title: cli.py: slide add, delete, and move commands
description: Wire `pypptx slide add`, `pypptx slide delete`, and `pypptx slide move` into the `slide` subgroup. Validate that exactly one of `--duplicate`/`--layout` is provided for `add` (fail with stderr message and exit code 1 otherwise). Call the corresponding `ops.slides` write functions. Format output via `output_result()`.
acceptance_criteria: `pypptx slide add deck.pptx --duplicate 1` outputs `{"added_file": "slideN.xml", "position": N}`; resulting file opens correctly.
`pypptx slide add deck.pptx --layout 1 --position 2` inserts at position 2 and outputs correct JSON.
`pypptx slide add` with both or neither `--duplicate`/`--layout` writes an error to stderr and exits non-zero.
`pypptx slide move deck.pptx 3 1` outputs `{"file": "slide3.xml", "from": 3, "to": 1}`; slide order is correct.
All three commands support `--plain` for human-readable output.
Errors go to stderr; exit code is non-zero on failure.

handoff_summary.completed: Added `slide_add_cmd`, `slide_delete_cmd`, and `slide_move_cmd` to `pypptx/cli.py` — all commands are registered under the `slide` subgroup, call the correct `ops.slides` functions, validate inputs, format output via `output_result()`, and respect `--plain`.
handoff_summary.risks: The `slide add --position` flow calls `add_slide` (which unpacks/repacks for .pptx files) then `move_slide` (a second unpack/repack cycle). This is two I/O passes on the file but is correct; a potential optimisation could do both in a single pptx_edit context, but that is out of scope for this bead.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should validate the three commands against the acceptance criteria using a real .pptx fixture.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — only `pypptx/cli.py` was modified; no other in-flight beads claim this file.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Output keys for `slide add` are `added_file`/`position` (matching acceptance criteria) rather than the `file`/`index` keys returned internally by `add_slide`. The `--position` option triggers a second `move_slide` call after the initial add, keeping implementation simple at the cost of two unpack/repack cycles for .pptx inputs.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: No tests run (tester agent responsibility). Acceptance criteria cover: duplicate add, layout add with position, mutual-exclusion error, delete, move, --plain flag, stderr/exit-code behaviour.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Adding with `--position` on a .pptx file performs two unpack/repack cycles. This is functionally correct but less efficient than a single-pass implementation.

conflict_risks: None — only `pypptx/cli.py` was modified; no other in-flight beads claim this file.

execution_history.summary: Implemented `pypptx slide add`, `pypptx slide delete`, and `pypptx slide move` CLI commands in `pypptx/cli.py`. All three are wired into the existing `slide` subgroup and call the corresponding `ops.slides` public functions (`add_slide`, `delete_slide`, `move_slide`). The `add` command validates that exactly one of `--duplicate`/`--layout` is provided (stderr + exit 1 otherwise), supports an optional `--position` by calling `move_slide` after `add_slide`, and outputs `{"added_file": ..., "position": N}`. The `delete` command outputs `{"deleted_file": ..., "deleted_index": N}`. The `move` command outputs `{"file": ..., "from": N, "to": N}`. All three support `--plain` for human-readable output and route errors to stderr with non-zero exit codes. Syntax verified with `uv run python -m py_compile`.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Python` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `python -m py_compile`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `pytest` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
